30: func newCensusJob(ctx context.Context, lurEnabled bool, r db.Reader, w db.Writer) (*censusJob, error) {
31: 	const op = "censusJob.newCensusJob"
32: 	switch {
33: 	case r == nil:
34: 		return nil, errors.New(ctx, errors.InvalidParameter, op, "missing db.Reader")
35: 	case w == nil:
36: 		return nil, errors.New(ctx, errors.InvalidParameter, op, "missing db.Writer")
37: 	}
38: 
39: 	return &censusJob{
40: 		r:                r,
41: 		w:                w,
42: 		lurEnabled:       lurEnabled,
43: 		emitEvents:       false,
44: 		sessionsAgent:    nil,
45: 		activeUsersAgent: nil,
46: 		eventCtx:         ctx,
47: 	}, nil
48: }

50: // Status reports the job’s current status.
51: func (c *censusJob) Status() scheduler.JobStatus {
52: 	return scheduler.JobStatus{}
53: }
54: 
55: // Run performs the required work depending on the implementation.
56: // The context is used to notify the job that it should exit early.
57: func (c *censusJob) Run(ctx context.Context) error {
58: 	err := RunFn(ctx, c)
59: 	return err
60: }
61: 
62: func runInternal(ctx context.Context, c *censusJob) error {
63: 	return nil
64: }
65: 
66: // NextRunIn returns the duration until the next job run should be scheduled.
67: // Census will run every hour to ensure any interrupted jobs will be re-attempted
68: func (c *censusJob) NextRunIn(_ context.Context) (time.Duration, error) {
69: 	return time.Hour, nil
70: }
71: 
72: // Name is the unique name of the job.
73: func (c *censusJob) Name() string {
74: 	return "job_run_census"
75: }
76:

78: func (c *censusJob) Description() string {
79: 	return "Gathers and exports session usage metrics"

6: import (

13: // Job defines an interface for jobs that can be invoked by the scheduler.
14: // Multiple goroutines may invoke methods on a Job simultaneously.
15: type Job interface {
16: 	// Status reports the job’s current status. It is called periodically
17: 	// while the Run method is running and immediately after the Run method
18: 	// completes.
19: 	//
20: 	// The scheduler uses the values in the returned JobStatus to verify
21: 	// that progress is being made by the job. The scheduler will interrupt
22: 	// the job if the values returned by Status do not change over a
23: 	// configurable amount of time.
24: 	//
25: 	// See scheduler.WithInterruptThreshold for more information.
26: 	Status() JobStatus
27: 
28: 	// Run starts the specified job and waits for it to complete.
29: 	// The context is used to notify the job that it should exit early.
30: 	//
31: 	// If the returned error is not nil, the job will be scheduled to run again
32: 	// immediately and the returned error will be logged.
33: 	Run(ctx context.Context) error
34: 
35: 	// NextRunIn returns the duration the job scheduler should wait before
36: 	// running the job again. It is only called after the Run method has
37: 	// completed and returned a nil error. If a zero duration is returned
38: 	// the job will be scheduled to run again immediately.
39: 	//
40: 	// If an error is returned, the error will be logged but the returned
41: 	// duration will still be used in scheduling.
42: 	NextRunIn(context.Context) (time.Duration, error)
43: 
44: 	// Name is the unique name of the job.
45: 	Name() string
46: 
47: 	// Description is the human readable description of the job.
48: 	Description() string
49: }
50: 
51: // A JobStatus represents the status of a job.
52: // Completed and Total are used to indicate job progress.
53: // The Completed value cannot be greater than the Total value
54: // and both values must be greater than or equal to zero.
55: //
56: // The scheduler uses the values in the JobStatus to verify that
57: // progress is being made by the job. The scheduler will interrupt the job
58: // if the values returned by Status do not change over a configurable
59: // amount of time.
60: type JobStatus struct {
61: 	// The job's work items
62: 	Completed int // number of items processed
63: 	Total     int // total number of items to be processed
64: }
65: 
66: func validateJob(ctx context.Context, j Job) error {
67: 	const op = "scheduler.validateJob"
68: 	if j == nil {

213: func (s *Scheduler) schedule(ctx context.Context, wg *sync.WaitGroup) {
214: 	const op = "scheduler.(Scheduler).schedule"
215: 	repo, err := s.jobRepoFn()
216: 	if err != nil {

222: 	if err != nil {

225: 	}
226: 
227: 	for _, r := range runs {

232: 				event.WriteError(ctx, op, inner, event.WithInfoMsg("error updating failed job run"))
233: 			}
234: 		}
235: 	}
236: }

238: func (s *Scheduler) runJob(ctx context.Context, wg *sync.WaitGroup, r *job.Run) error {
239: 	const op = "scheduler.(Scheduler).runJob"
240: 	regJob, ok := s.registeredJobs.Load(r.JobName)
241: 	if !ok {
242: 		return fmt.Errorf("job %q not registered on scheduler", r.JobName)
243: 	}
244: 
245: 	repo, err := s.jobRepoFn()
246: 	if err != nil {
247: 		return fmt.Errorf("failed to create job repository: %w", err)
248: 	}
249: 
250: 	j := regJob.(Job)
251: 	rj := &runningJob{runId: r.PrivateId, status: j.Status}
252: 	_, loaded := s.runningJobs.LoadOrStore(r.JobName, rj)
253: 	if loaded {
254: 		return fmt.Errorf("job %q is already running", r.JobName)
255: 	}
256: 	var jobContext context.Context
257: 	jobContext, rj.cancelCtx = context.WithCancel(ctx)
258: 
259: 	wg.Add(1)
260: 	go func() {
261: 		defer rj.cancelCtx()
262: 		defer wg.Done()
263: 		runErr := j.Run(jobContext)
264: 
265: 		// Get final status report to update run progress with
266: 		status := j.Status()
267: 		var updateErr error
268: 		switch {
269: 		case ctx.Err() != nil:
270: 			// Base context is no longer valid, skip repo updates as they will fail and exit
271: 		case runErr == nil:
272: 			nextRun, inner := j.NextRunIn(ctx)
273: 			if inner != nil {
274: 				event.WriteError(ctx, op, inner, event.WithInfoMsg("error getting next run time", "name", j.Name()))
275: 			}
276: 			_, updateErr = repo.CompleteRun(ctx, r.PrivateId, nextRun, status.Completed, status.Total)
277: 		default:
278: 			event.WriteError(ctx, op, runErr, event.WithInfoMsg("job run failed", "run id", r.PrivateId, "name", j.Name()))
279: 			_, updateErr = repo.FailRun(ctx, r.PrivateId, status.Completed, status.Total)
280: 		}
281: 
282: 		if updateErr != nil {
283: 			event.WriteError(ctx, op, updateErr, event.WithInfoMsg("error updating job run", "name", j.Name()))
284: 		}
285: 		s.runningJobs.Delete(j.Name())
286: 	}()
287:

289: }

295: 		"monitor interval", s.monitorInterval.String(),
296: 		"interrupt threshold", s.interruptThreshold.String())
297: 	timer := time.NewTimer(0)

324: 			}
325: 		}

327: 	}

30: func TestSessionConnectionCleanupJob(t *testing.T) {
31: 	t.Parallel()
32: 	ctx := context.Background()
33: 
34: 	gracePeriod := new(atomic.Int64)
35: 	gracePeriod.Store(int64(time.Second))
36: 
37: 	require, assert := require.New(t), assert.New(t)
38: 	conn, _ := db.TestSetup(t, "postgres")
39: 	rw := db.New(conn)
40: 	wrapper := db.TestWrapper(t)
41: 	iamRepo := iam.TestRepo(t, conn, wrapper)
42: 	kms := kms.TestKms(t, conn, wrapper)
43: 	serversRepo, err := server.NewRepository(ctx, rw, rw, kms)
44: 	require.NoError(err)
45: 	sessionRepo, err := NewRepository(ctx, rw, rw, kms)
46: 	require.NoError(err)
47: 	connectionRepo, err := NewConnectionRepository(ctx, rw, rw, kms)
48: 	require.NoError(err)
49: 
50: 	numConns := 12
51: 
52: 	// Create two "workers". One will remain untouched while the other "goes
53: 	// away and comes back" (worker 2).
54: 	worker1 := server.TestKmsWorker(t, conn, wrapper)
55: 	worker2 := server.TestKmsWorker(t, conn, wrapper)
56: 
57: 	// Create a few sessions on each, activate, and authorize a connection
58: 	var connIds []string
59: 	connIdsByWorker := make(map[string][]string)
60: 	for i := 0; i < numConns; i++ {
61: 		serverId := worker1.PublicId
62: 		if i%2 == 0 {
63: 			serverId = worker2.PublicId
64: 		}
65: 		sess := TestDefaultSession(t, conn, wrapper, iamRepo, WithDbOpts(db.WithSkipVetForWrite(true)))
66: 		sess, _, err = sessionRepo.ActivateSession(ctx, sess.GetPublicId(), sess.Version, []byte("foo"))
67: 		require.NoError(err)
68: 		c, _, err := AuthorizeConnection(ctx, sessionRepo, connectionRepo, sess.GetPublicId(), serverId)
69: 		require.NoError(err)
70: 		require.Equal(StatusAuthorized, ConnectionStatusFromString(c.Status))
71: 		connIds = append(connIds, c.GetPublicId())
72: 		if i%2 == 0 {
73: 			connIdsByWorker[worker2.PublicId] = append(connIdsByWorker[worker2.PublicId], c.GetPublicId())
74: 		} else {
75: 			connIdsByWorker[worker1.PublicId] = append(connIdsByWorker[worker1.PublicId], c.GetPublicId())
76: 		}
77: 	}
78: 
79: 	// Mark half of the connections connected and leave the others authorized.
80: 	// This is just to ensure we have a spread when we test it out.
81: 	for i, connId := range connIds {
82: 		if i%2 == 0 {
83: 			cc, err := connectionRepo.ConnectConnection(ctx, ConnectWith{
84: 				ConnectionId:       connId,
85: 				ClientTcpAddress:   "127.0.0.1",
86: 				ClientTcpPort:      22,
87: 				EndpointTcpAddress: "127.0.0.1",
88: 				EndpointTcpPort:    22,
89: 				UserClientIp:       "127.0.0.1",
90: 			})
91: 			require.NoError(err)
92: 			require.Equal(StatusConnected, ConnectionStatusFromString(cc.Status))
93: 		}
94: 	}
95: 
96: 	// Create the job.
97: 	job, err := newSessionConnectionCleanupJob(ctx, rw, gracePeriod)
98: 	job.workerStatusGracePeriod = gracePeriod // by-pass factory assert so we dont have to wait so long
99: 	require.NoError(err)
100: 
101: 	// sleep the status grace period.
102: 	time.Sleep(time.Duration(gracePeriod.Load()))
103: 
104: 	// Push an upsert to the first worker so that its status has been
105: 	// updated.
106: 	_, err = serversRepo.UpsertWorkerStatus(ctx, server.NewWorker(scope.Global.String(),
107: 		server.WithName(worker1.GetName()),
108: 		server.WithAddress(worker1.GetAddress())),
109: 		server.WithPublicId(worker1.GetPublicId()))
110: 	require.NoError(err)
111: 
112: 	// Run the job.
113: 	require.NoError(job.Run(ctx))
114: 
115: 	// Assert connection state on both workers.
116: 	assertConnections := func(workerId string, closed bool) {
117: 		connIds, ok := connIdsByWorker[workerId]
118: 		require.True(ok)
119: 		require.Len(connIds, 6)
120: 		for _, connId := range connIds {
121: 			conn, err := connectionRepo.LookupConnection(ctx, connId)
122: 			require.NoError(err)
123: 			var foundClosed bool
124: 			if ConnectionStatusFromString(conn.Status) == StatusClosed {
125: 				foundClosed = true
126: 			}
127: 			assert.Equal(closed, foundClosed)
128: 		}
129: 	}
130: 
131: 	// Assert that all connections on the second worker are closed
132: 	assertConnections(worker2.PublicId, true)
133: 	// Assert that all connections on the first worker are still open
134: 	assertConnections(worker1.PublicId, false)
135: }

177: func TestCloseConnectionsForDeadWorkers(t *testing.T) {
178: 	t.Parallel()
179: 	ctx := context.Background()
180: 	require := require.New(t)
181: 	conn, _ := db.TestSetup(t, "postgres")
182: 	rw := db.New(conn)
183: 	wrapper := db.TestWrapper(t)
184: 	iamRepo := iam.TestRepo(t, conn, wrapper)
185: 	kms := kms.TestKms(t, conn, wrapper)
186: 	repo, err := NewRepository(ctx, rw, rw, kms)
187: 	require.NoError(err)
188: 	gracePeriod := 1 * time.Second
189: 	connRepo, err := NewConnectionRepository(ctx, rw, rw, kms)
190: 	require.NoError(err)
191: 	serversRepo, err := server.NewRepository(ctx, rw, rw, kms)
192: 	require.NoError(err)
193: 
194: 	defaultLiveness := new(atomic.Int64)
195: 	defaultLiveness.Store(int64(server.DefaultLiveness))
196: 
197: 	job, err := newSessionConnectionCleanupJob(ctx, rw, defaultLiveness)
198: 	require.NoError(err)
199: 
200: 	// connection count = 6 * states(authorized, connected, closed = 3) * servers_with_open_connections(3)
201: 	numConns := 54
202: 
203: 	// Create four "workers". This is similar to the setup in
204: 	// TestRepository_CloseDeadConnectionsOnWorker, but a bit more complex;
205: 	// firstly, the last worker will have no connections at all, and we will be
206: 	// closing the others in stages to test multiple servers being closed at
207: 	// once.
208: 	worker1 := server.TestKmsWorker(t, conn, wrapper)
209: 	worker2 := server.TestKmsWorker(t, conn, wrapper)
210: 	worker3 := server.TestKmsWorker(t, conn, wrapper)
211: 	worker4 := server.TestKmsWorker(t, conn, wrapper)
212: 
213: 	// Create sessions on the first three, activate, and authorize connections
214: 	var worker1ConnIds, worker2ConnIds, worker3ConnIds []string
215: 	for i := 0; i < numConns; i++ {
216: 		var serverId string
217: 		if i%3 == 0 {
218: 			serverId = worker1.PublicId
219: 		} else if i%3 == 1 {
220: 			serverId = worker2.PublicId
221: 		} else {
222: 			serverId = worker3.PublicId
223: 		}
224: 		sess := TestDefaultSession(t, conn, wrapper, iamRepo, WithDbOpts(db.WithSkipVetForWrite(true)))
225: 		sess, _, err = repo.ActivateSession(ctx, sess.GetPublicId(), sess.Version, []byte("foo"))
226: 		require.NoError(err)
227: 		c, err := connRepo.AuthorizeConnection(ctx, sess.GetPublicId(), serverId)
228: 		require.NoError(err)
229: 		require.Equal(StatusAuthorized, ConnectionStatusFromString(c.Status))
230: 		if i%3 == 0 {
231: 			worker1ConnIds = append(worker1ConnIds, c.GetPublicId())
232: 		} else if i%3 == 1 {
233: 			worker2ConnIds = append(worker2ConnIds, c.GetPublicId())
234: 		} else {
235: 			worker3ConnIds = append(worker3ConnIds, c.GetPublicId())
236: 		}
237: 	}
238: 
239: 	// Mark a third of the connections connected, a third closed, and leave the
240: 	// others authorized. This is just to ensure we have a spread when we test it
241: 	// out.
242: 	for i, connId := range func() []string {
243: 		var s []string
244: 		s = append(s, worker1ConnIds...)
245: 		s = append(s, worker2ConnIds...)
246: 		s = append(s, worker3ConnIds...)
247: 		return s
248: 	}() {
249: 		if i%3 == 0 {
250: 			cc, err := connRepo.ConnectConnection(ctx, ConnectWith{
251: 				ConnectionId:       connId,
252: 				ClientTcpAddress:   "127.0.0.1",
253: 				ClientTcpPort:      22,
254: 				EndpointTcpAddress: "127.0.0.1",
255: 				EndpointTcpPort:    22,
256: 				UserClientIp:       "127.0.0.1",
257: 			})
258: 			require.NoError(err)
259: 			require.Equal(StatusConnected, ConnectionStatusFromString(cc.Status))
260: 		} else if i%3 == 1 {
261: 			resp, err := connRepo.closeConnections(ctx, []CloseWith{
262: 				{
263: 					ConnectionId: connId,
264: 					ClosedReason: ConnectionCanceled,
265: 				},
266: 			})
267: 			require.NoError(err)
268: 			require.Len(resp, 1)
269: 			cs := resp[0].ConnectionState
270: 			require.Equal(StatusClosed, cs)
271: 		}
272: 	}
273: 
274: 	// updateServer is a helper for updating the update time for our
275: 	// servers. The controller is read back so that we can reference
276: 	// the most up-to-date fields.
277: 	updateServer := func(t *testing.T, w *server.Worker) *server.Worker {
278: 		t.Helper()
279: 		pubId := w.GetPublicId()
280: 		w.PublicId = ""
281: 		wkr, err := serversRepo.UpsertWorkerStatus(ctx, w, server.WithPublicId(pubId))
282: 		require.NoError(err)
283: 		return wkr
284: 	}
285: 
286: 	// requireConnectionStatus is a helper expecting all connections on a worker
287: 	// to be closed.
288: 	requireConnectionStatus := func(t *testing.T, connIds []string, expectAllClosed bool) {
289: 		t.Helper()
290: 
291: 		var conns []*Connection
292: 		require.NoError(rw.SearchWhere(ctx, &conns, "", nil))
293: 		for i, connId := range connIds {
294: 			var expected ConnectionStatus
295: 			switch {
296: 			case expectAllClosed:
297: 				expected = StatusClosed
298: 
299: 			case i%3 == 0:
300: 				expected = StatusConnected
301: 
302: 			case i%3 == 1:
303: 				expected = StatusClosed
304: 
305: 			case i%3 == 2:
306: 				expected = StatusAuthorized
307: 			}
308: 
309: 			conn, err := connRepo.LookupConnection(ctx, connId)
310: 			require.NoError(err)
311: 			require.Equal(expected, ConnectionStatusFromString(conn.Status), "expected latest status for %q (index %d) to be %v", connId, i, expected)
312: 		}
313: 	}
314: 
315: 	// We need this helper to fix the zone on protobuf timestamps
316: 	// versus what gets reported in the
317: 	// closeConnectionsForDeadWorkersResult.
318: 	timestampPbAsUTC := func(t *testing.T, tm time.Time) time.Time {
319: 		t.Helper()
320: 		// utcLoc, err := time.LoadLocation("Etc/UTC")
321: 		// require.NoError(err)
322: 		return tm.In(time.Local)
323: 	}
324: 
325: 	// Now try some scenarios.
326: 	{
327: 		// Now, try the basis, or where all workers are reporting in.
328: 		worker1 = updateServer(t, worker1)
329: 		worker2 = updateServer(t, worker2)
330: 		worker3 = updateServer(t, worker3)
331: 		updateServer(t, worker4) // no re-assignment here because we never reference the server again
332: 
333: 		result, err := job.closeConnectionsForDeadWorkers(ctx, gracePeriod)
334: 		require.NoError(err)
335: 		require.Empty(result)
336: 		// Expect appropriate split connection state on worker1
337: 		requireConnectionStatus(t, worker1ConnIds, false)
338: 		// Expect appropriate split connection state on worker2
339: 		requireConnectionStatus(t, worker2ConnIds, false)
340: 		// Expect appropriate split connection state on worker3
341: 		requireConnectionStatus(t, worker3ConnIds, false)
342: 	}
343: 
344: 	{
345: 		// Now try a zero case - similar to the basis, but only in that no results
346: 		// are expected to be returned for workers with no connections, even if
347: 		// they are dead. Here, the server with no connections is worker #4.
348: 		time.Sleep(gracePeriod)
349: 		worker1 = updateServer(t, worker1)
350: 		worker2 = updateServer(t, worker2)
351: 		worker3 = updateServer(t, worker3)
352: 
353: 		result, err := job.closeConnectionsForDeadWorkers(ctx, gracePeriod)
354: 		require.NoError(err)
355: 		require.Empty(result)
356: 		// Expect appropriate split connection state on worker1
357: 		requireConnectionStatus(t, worker1ConnIds, false)
358: 		// Expect appropriate split connection state on worker2
359: 		requireConnectionStatus(t, worker2ConnIds, false)
360: 		// Expect appropriate split connection state on worker3
361: 		requireConnectionStatus(t, worker3ConnIds, false)
362: 	}
363: 
364: 	{
365: 		// The first induction is letting the first worker "die" by not updating it
366: 		// too. All of its authorized and connected connections should be dead.
367: 		time.Sleep(gracePeriod)
368: 		worker2 = updateServer(t, worker2)
369: 		worker3 = updateServer(t, worker3)
370: 
371: 		result, err := job.closeConnectionsForDeadWorkers(ctx, gracePeriod)
372: 		require.NoError(err)
373: 		// Assert that we have one result with the appropriate ID and
374: 		// number of connections closed. Due to how things are
375: 		require.Equal([]closeConnectionsForDeadWorkersResult{
376: 			{
377: 				WorkerId:                worker1.PublicId,
378: 				LastUpdateTime:          timestampPbAsUTC(t, worker1.GetLastStatusTime().AsTime()),
379: 				NumberConnectionsClosed: 12, // 18 per server, with 6 closed already
380: 			},
381: 		}, result)
382: 		// Expect all connections closed on worker1
383: 		requireConnectionStatus(t, worker1ConnIds, true)
384: 		// Expect appropriate split connection state on worker2
385: 		requireConnectionStatus(t, worker2ConnIds, false)
386: 		// Expect appropriate split connection state on worker3
387: 		requireConnectionStatus(t, worker3ConnIds, false)
388: 	}
389: 
390: 	{
391: 		// The final case is having the other two workers die. After
392: 		// this, we should have all connections closed with the
393: 		// appropriate message from the next two servers acted on.
394: 		time.Sleep(gracePeriod)
395: 
396: 		result, err := job.closeConnectionsForDeadWorkers(ctx, gracePeriod)
397: 		require.NoError(err)
398: 		// Assert that we have one result with the appropriate ID and number of connections closed.
399: 		require.ElementsMatch([]closeConnectionsForDeadWorkersResult{
400: 			{
401: 				WorkerId:                worker2.PublicId,
402: 				LastUpdateTime:          timestampPbAsUTC(t, worker2.GetLastStatusTime().AsTime()),
403: 				NumberConnectionsClosed: 12, // 18 per server, with 6 closed already
404: 			},
405: 			{
406: 				WorkerId:                worker3.PublicId,
407: 				LastUpdateTime:          timestampPbAsUTC(t, worker3.GetLastStatusTime().AsTime()),
408: 				NumberConnectionsClosed: 12, // 18 per server, with 6 closed already
409: 			},
410: 		}, result)
411: 		// Expect all connections closed on worker1
412: 		requireConnectionStatus(t, worker1ConnIds, true)
413: 		// Expect all connections closed on worker2
414: 		requireConnectionStatus(t, worker2ConnIds, true)
415: 		// Expect all connections closed on worker3
416: 		requireConnectionStatus(t, worker3ConnIds, true)
417: 	}
418: }

420: func TestCloseWorkerlessConnections(t *testing.T) {
421: 	t.Parallel()
422: 	ctx := context.Background()
423: 	require := require.New(t)
